title: ChromaLab Knowledge Seed v2
description: Expanded curated offline-first knowledge pack for chromatogram terminology, OCR/text classification, report caveats, safe VLM grounding, and compound-class stubs. It contains no measured sample RT, peak area, spectrum, or proprietary library data.

sourceRefs.label: ChromaLab curated chromatography safety rules v2
sourceRefs.citation: Internal conservative rules derived from ChromaLab Phase 0-6C evidence gates.
sourceRefs.notes: Primary source for text classification rules, release caveats, and safe prompt snippets.

sourceRefs.label: W3C PROV-O
sourceRefs.notes: Used to align knowledge and report provenance vocabulary.

sourceRefs.label: ChEBI, Chemical Entities of Biological Interest
sourceRefs.citation: EMBL-EBI ChEBI data, CC BY 4.0 subject to EMBL-EBI terms.
sourceRefs.notes: Permitted candidate for future curated chemical synonyms after attribution and source snapshot review.

sourceRefs.label: PubChem download and provenance policy
sourceRefs.citation: PubChem download documentation and NCBI/NLM policies.
sourceRefs.license: NCBI/NLM policies plus contributor-specific source terms
sourceRefs.notes: Use only through reviewed source definitions until contributor-level licenses are checked.

sourceRefs.label: NIST Chemistry WebBook / AMDIS / SRD policy review
sourceRefs.citation: NIST Chemistry WebBook, Standard Reference Database 69.
sourceRefs.license: NIST Standard Reference Data Program terms; bundling requires explicit product review
sourceRefs.notes: Referenced for policy and caveats only here; no NIST spectral, RI, AMDIS, or WebBook database data is bundled.

entries.shortText: A chromatogram is a signal trace plotted against retention coordinate, usually time, and must be interpreted through calibrated axes and trace evidence.
Retention time (RT) is the calibrated X coordinate of a chromatographic event, normally reported at the peak apex after X-axis calibration.
A chromatographic peak is a local signal feature with apex, boundaries, baseline context, and integration evidence.
Peak apex is the local maximum point of a detected peak on the extracted trace.
Peak area is the deterministic integration result between accepted boundaries; knowledge entries cannot create it.
Area percent is a derived relative value based on measured peak areas and report policy.
Abundance is a Y-axis detector signal magnitude, not a compound identity.
Intensity is detector signal magnitude and must be linked to Y calibration before release-quality metric claims.
Baseline is the local signal background used by deterministic integration and quality review.
Baseline drift is slow background movement that can affect integration boundaries and should trigger review when severe.
Noise is local signal variation used for quality review and S/N calculations when measured.
RMS noise is a measured noise estimate; it must not be inferred from glossary knowledge.
S/N is signal-to-noise ratio and requires measured signal and measured noise evidence.
FWHM is full width at half maximum and must come from measured trace and peak evidence.
Peak width describes the span of a peak under an explicit width definition such as FWHM or integration bounds.
Integration boundaries delimit deterministic area calculation and must be visible in evidence when reported.
A shoulder peak is a partially resolved feature on the side of a larger peak and normally requires review.
Overlapping peaks share signal and require boundary/provenance caveats before release-quality integration.
Coelution means two or more compounds or chromatographic features elute together and cannot be separated by image evidence alone.
Resolution describes chromatographic separation quality; it is not inferred without peak width and spacing evidence.
Tailing or asymmetry is a peak-shape review flag and should not be converted into a compound identity.
A total ion chromatogram summarizes total ion current over retention time.
An extracted ion chromatogram shows signal from a selected m/z or mass window.
Selected ion monitoring tracks chosen ion channels and does not by itself identify compounds.
SCAN mode records mass spectra over a range and may accompany TIC data, but report identities still require evidence.
m/z is mass-to-charge ratio; values in ion/channel headings are channel metadata, not retention-time labels.
An extracted ion chromatogram is channel-specific trace evidence, not standalone compound identification.
Selected ion monitoring panels must preserve channel provenance and confidence limits.
Ion labels identify a mass channel or ion selection and must not be treated as peak annotations.
Ion/channel text such as Ion 71.00 (70.70 to 71.70) names a signal channel, not a peak RT.
Mass range values in parentheses describe the m/z extraction window and are not retention times.
The X-axis maps pixel position to retention coordinate after calibration.
The Y-axis maps pixel position to abundance or intensity after calibration.
Tick labels may calibrate an axis only when linked to deterministic tick geometry.
Axis labels describe units or signal meaning and are not peak labels.
Plot area is the coordinate rectangle containing trace/grid/frame content, excluding titles and labels.
Graph panel is the full chart block, including title/channel text, axes, tick labels, and plot area.
Title/header text describes the graph or channel and must not become peak evidence.
Legend text labels series or channels and is not a peak annotation.
Grid lines are visual guides and must be suppressed before trace or peak evidence is accepted.
A calibration anchor pairs deterministic pixel geometry with an OCR/user value; text alone is not enough.
Kovats index requires valid reference-series retention data and cannot be calculated from glossary knowledge.
Retention index is a derived chromatographic value requiring method-compatible reference evidence.
n-alkane reference series entries define the RI scale, but measured reference RTs must come from the same method or run family.
C10-C40 labels follow straight-chain saturated hydrocarbon naming; this is a naming stub only.
RI interpolation must use adjacent reference alkanes around the target peak and preserve the method context.
If the reference series is missing or not method-compatible, RI/Kovats must be omitted or marked unavailable.

entries.canonicalLabel: Ion/channel title is not peak label
entries.shortText: Ion/channel title text, including numeric m/z windows, is TITLE_OR_CHANNEL and must not create PeakLabelEvidence.

entries.canonicalLabel: m/z value is not retention time
entries.shortText: An m/z value or mass range number is not a retention-time peak label.

entries.canonicalLabel: Mass range number is not peak RT
entries.shortText: Numbers inside an ion/mass range are channel metadata and must be classified away from peak annotation.

entries.canonicalLabel: Title/header numbers are not peak annotations
entries.shortText: Numbers in title/header regions are graph metadata unless local plot and signal verification proves otherwise.
Peak annotations require plot proximity and local signal verification before they can seed a peak review candidate.
Tick labels participate in calibration only when linked to deterministic tick pixel positions.

entries.canonicalLabel: Axis label is not peak label
entries.shortText: Axis labels such as Abundance, Intensity, or Retention Time must not become peak annotations.

entries.canonicalLabel: Legend text is not peak label
entries.shortText: Legend/series text labels signals or channels and cannot create peak evidence.
No release-quality RT, height, area, FWHM, or S/N claim is allowed without valid calibration evidence.

entries.canonicalLabel: Trace evidence required for peak metrics
entries.shortText: Peak metrics require linked trace, boundaries, and peak evidence; knowledge cannot fill missing trace evidence.

entries.canonicalLabel: No compound assignment without explicit evidence
entries.shortText: Compound assignment requires explicit library, spectrum, retention-index, method, or user-confirmed evidence.
Kovats/RI must not be reported as calculated without valid same-method reference-series data.
Peaks with weak, shoulder, overlap, sparse-trace, or low-S/N evidence must be clearly labelled review-grade.

entries.canonicalLabel: VLM explanation is semantic assistance only
entries.shortText: VLM may explain warnings and classify text, but deterministic CV/math owns numeric measurements.
Image-derived chromatogram analysis must expose calibration, trace, OCR, and peak evidence limits.
Knowledge can explain and classify; it cannot fabricate RT, height, area, FWHM, S/N, baseline, Kovats, or integration boundaries.
Alkane is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
N-Alkane is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Hydrocarbon is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Aromatic Hydrocarbon is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Biomarker is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Sterane is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Terpane is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Pah is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Compound Class is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.
Unresolved Complex Mixture is a chemical/domain class term; it cannot identify a sample peak without explicit evidence.

Use this snippet to explain sparse trace warning with evidence and without creating numeric metrics.
Use this snippet to explain peak overlap warning with evidence and without creating numeric metrics.
Use this snippet to explain shoulder peak warning with evidence and without creating numeric metrics.
Use this snippet to explain OCR ambiguity warning with evidence and without creating numeric metrics.
Use this snippet to explain VLM disagreement warning with evidence and without creating numeric metrics.
Use this snippet to explain diagnostic-only report explanation with evidence and without creating numeric metrics.
